name: csctf
description: Chat Shared Conversation To File - Convert ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude share links to clean Markdown + HTML transcripts. Preserves code fences with language detection, deterministic filenames, GitHub Pages publishing. Bun-native CLI.
capabilities: Convert share links from ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude to Markdown
Preserve code fences with language detection and syntax highlighting
Generate deterministic, collision-proof filenames from conversation titles
Create static HTML output with inline CSS and zero JavaScript
Publish exports directly to GitHub Pages with one command
Handle Claude.ai Cloudflare protection using your Chrome session
author: Dicklesworthstone
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill describing a CLI tool for converting AI chat transcripts. All 155 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation examples showing shell commands, legitimate hardcoded URLs for the tool's own distribution, standard CLI installation patterns like pipe-to-shell, and documented browser automation for Claude.ai authentication. The skill contains no executable code and describes a legitimate productivity tool with documented security measures.
valueStatement: Saving AI chat transcripts often breaks code blocks and creates messy filenames. This tool converts share links from ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude into clean Markdown and HTML with preserved formatting and syntax highlighting.
userTitle: Convert AI Chat Share Links to Markdown
limitations: Requires public share links (Claude.ai uses your browser session)
Provider layouts may change over time affecting selectors
Markdown and HTML exports require share link to be accessible

useCases.title: Save Code-Rich Chats
useCases.description: Archive conversations containing code examples with syntax highlighting preserved for future reference.

useCases.title: Build AI Conversation Dataset
useCases.description: Collect and organize AI interactions for analysis, citation, or training data preparation.

useCases.title: Create Documentation from Chats
useCases.description: Transform AI-generated content into clean, self-contained HTML documentation ready for hosting.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Conversion
promptTemplates.scenario: Convert a share link to Markdown
promptTemplates.prompt: Save this Claude conversation to Markdown: https://claude.ai/share/549c846d-f6c8-411c-9039-a9a14db376cf

promptTemplates.title: HTML Output Only
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this ChatGPT link to HTML only: https://chatgpt.com/share/69343092-91ac-800b-996c-7552461b9b70

promptTemplates.title: Publish to Pages
promptTemplates.scenario: Export and publish to GitHub Pages
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this Grok conversation and publish to GitHub Pages: https://grok.com/share/bGVnYWN5_d5329c61-f497-40b7-9472-c555fa71af9c

bestPractices: Use public share links for ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok for fastest results
Increase timeout with --timeout-ms for large or slow-loading conversations
Run --check-updates periodically to get the latest improvements
Verify GitHub CLI authentication with gh auth status before publishing
antiPatterns: Using private share links - they are not accessible without proper authentication
Ignoring selector changes - update the tool when providers change layouts
Publishing without --dry-run first - verify the output before pushing

faq.question: Which AI platforms are supported?
faq.answer: ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, and Claude share links are all supported. Each has optimized extraction logic.

faq.question: Do I need a Claude.ai login for all platforms?
faq.answer: No. Only Claude.ai requires your Chrome session. ChatGPT, Gemini, and Grok use public share links.

faq.question: What file formats does it produce?
faq.answer: Two files are created: Markdown (.md) with preserved code fences and static HTML (.html) with inline styling.

faq.question: Is my data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: Only the share URL is fetched. GitHub publishing is opt-in and uses the official gh CLI for authentication.

faq.question: What happens if code blocks are not detected?
faq.answer: The tool uses provider-specific selectors with fallback chains. Report issues if certain content is missed.

faq.question: How is this different from copy-pasting?
faq.answer: Copy-paste often breaks code formatting and loses language hints. This tool preserves fences, syntax highlighting, and generates clean deterministic filenames.
